creator: pierre@freebsdfoundation.org
text:
(In reply to Michael Sinatra from comment #15)

From crypto/openssl/crypto/rsa/build.info:

```
IF[{- !$disabled{'deprecated-0.9.8'} -}]
  SOURCE[../../libcrypto]=rsa_depr.c
ENDIF
```
The decision when importing OpenSSL 3 in base was to avoid OpenSSL's APIs that were already deprecated in 1.1.1, updating software to contemporary APIs in the process.

Adding rsa_depr.c to the Makefile meant allowing some 0.9.8 functions to be reachable, but not all. We should definitely not remain in this in-between state. I am preparing a patch that will also add bn_depr.c, e_old.c, and dh_depr.c, which I believe should complete the support for OpenSSL's 0.9.8 API.
